name: Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón
name_original: Ruta de los XII Apóstoles — circular desde Salvaleón
region_intro: Hiking around Salvaleón, located in the south of Extremadura, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by rolling pastures and mountains. The region features prominent natural landmarks such as Risco Barbellío and Monsalud Mountain. Verdant streams wind through the landscape, and ancient cork oaks are a distinctive feature along many trails. These physical features provide varied experiences for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Salvaleón The most popular hiking route is Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón, a 9.97 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route incorporates single tracks and passes by the impressive Cork Oaks of the Twelve Apostles. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop, a moderate 9.18 miles (14.8 km) path. This trail explores the historic Nogales Castle and the scenic Nogales Reservoir. Local hikers also love the The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks – Trail of the Twelve Apostles loop from Salvaleón, a 8.46 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through pastures and ancient cork oak groves, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Salvaleón is defined by rolling pastures, mountains, and ancient cork oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Salvaleón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from a few reviews. More than dozens of hikers have used komoot to explore Salvaleón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Salvaleón?
A: Hiking around Salvaleón offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling pastures and mountains. You'll find prominent natural landmarks like Risco Barbellío and Monsalud Mountain. During spring, the area transforms with verdant streams winding through lush green pastures, and ancient cork oaks are a distinctive feature along many trails.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Salvaleón?
A: Yes, Salvaleón offers a few easier routes. While most trails are moderate, there are 2 routes classified as easy. For example, a shorter option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991621788/castillo-de-nogales-circular-by-los-ejidos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos&lt;/a&gt;, which is under 5 km and takes about 1 hour 20 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking in Salvaleón?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt;, ancient trees that are a highlight of several routes. Another significant feature is Risco Barbellío, a mountain with a massive rock at its summit housing a cave where vultures nest, offering breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2779557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Cliffs of Risco de Valbellido&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Salvaleón?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Salvaleón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219153536/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-salvaleon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 16 km long and takes around 4 hours 36 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Salvaleón?
A: The best time to experience Salvaleón's outdoor splendor is during spring. This is when the landscape is most vibrant, with numerous streams and 'paradisiac green pastures' making for a picturesque environment.

Q: Can I visit any historical sites or castles on the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222605650/nogales-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Salvaleón itself also has the ruins of its own castle, which, despite being destroyed, still offers ancient stone walls and 14th-century Arabic cisterns to explore.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Salvaleón?
A: The hiking routes in Salvaleón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from several reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from rolling pastures to mountains, and the distinctive ancient cork oak forests. The presence of historical sites like Nogales Castle also adds to the appeal.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging experience, there is at least one difficult route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219167772/route-of-the-xii-apostles-circular-from-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the XII Apostles — circular from Nogales&lt;/a&gt; is a longer option, spanning over 20 km and typically taking more than 5 hours to complete, with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that feature the famous Cork Oaks of the Twelve Apostles?
A: Yes, the ancient Cork Oaks of the Twelve Apostles are a distinctive feature along one of the most famous hiking routes. You can specifically experience them on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks – Trail of the Twelve Apostles loop from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 13.6 km long.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The region is home to diverse wildlife. Risco Barbellío is known for housing a cave where vultures nest, so you might spot these impressive birds. The natural environment of pastures and cork oak groves also supports various other species, though specific sightings depend on the time of year and trail.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly hikes in Salvaleón?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, the presence of easy and moderate routes suggests options for families. Shorter, less strenuous circular paths, especially those under 5 km, would be suitable for families with children. The natural beauty and historical sites can also make for engaging walks.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Salvaleón?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Salvaleón is a rural area, and it is generally expected that parking is available in or near the town centers or designated starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check local information or maps for specific parking areas before your hike.
tips: Route the 12 Apostles. This route originates as an activity of the Nature Classroom of the Popular University of Salvaleón. It is a circular route of 16.5km, of moderate difficulty.
